intelligence.name: Support Ticket Analyzer
intelligence.description: Analyzes customer support tickets for sentiment, churn risk, and actionable insights

frustration_level.rationale: 0-10 provides sufficient granularity without overwhelming the model with choice paralysis. Aligns with NPS-style scoring familiar to business users.

churn_risk.rationale: 0-10 matches frustration scale for cognitive consistency. Threshold of ≥7 empirically captures actionable churn signals.

confidence.rationale: 0.0-1.0 is standard probability range. Enables filtering low-confidence outputs for human review.

categories.rationale: Pre-defined categories enable consistent aggregation. 'other' provides escape hatch for edge cases.

sentiment.rationale: 'frustrated' separated from 'negative' because it's a stronger churn signal requiring different handling.

ticket_volume.note: Batch processing at 10 tickets/call with 1s delay = 600 tickets/10min max throughput

cost_model.note: Based on Claude 3.5 Sonnet pricing. Does not include infrastructure costs.

data_retention.note: No automatic cleanup. Database grows unbounded. Manual archival required.

multi_tenancy.note: No row-level security. Application-level isolation only. organization_id in URL = trust-based.

timezone.note: No timezone-aware date handling. Weekly reports use Monday-Sunday UTC.

language.note: Prompts in English. No multilingual support. Non-English tickets may produce degraded results.

critique.rationale: Second pass catches inconsistencies (e.g., churn_risk=9 but sentiment=positive) and validates reasoning quality.
